tracks.meaning: to drive a nail or pointed object forcefully into something
a sharp pointed projection on the body of a plant or animal, such as a thorn or quill
a sudden rapid increase in the number of cases or instances of something, especially a disease
a sharp pointed crystalline form or structure
a sudden sharp increase in volume, intensity, or prominence of sound or signal
a sudden rapid increase in data traffic, usage, or activity on a network or system
a sudden sharp increase in the price or value of a stock, commodity, or asset
a thin sharp piece or skewer, typically used for serving appetizers or securing food
a sharp pointed rocky formation or pinnacle projecting from the ground
to inject a narcotic drug, typically heroin, intravenously
a unit of measurement equal to 1.25 millimeters or a similar small increment
a type of fortification or defensive structure with projecting points
to move upward or rise suddenly and sharply
a deliberate hitting of the ball downward with force in volleyball or similar sports
to hit a ball downward sharply and forcefully, especially in volleyball or badminton
a sudden surge of electrical voltage or power in a circuit or system
a sudden sharp increase in wind speed or intensity
